name: Pierogi (Peer-o-gee)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mound flour on a work surface and make a well in the center. Crack eggs and salt into the well. Add water, gradually incorporating the flour from the sides into the liquid at the center using one hand while keeping the rest of the flour mounded with the other. Knead until the dough is firm.
2. Cover the dough with a warm cloth for 10 minutes.
3. Divide the dough into two halves. On a floured surface, work with one half at a time, roll out the dough as thin as possible.
4. Cut 3-inch rounds using a large biscuit cutter (or similar). Place a small spoonful of filling onto each round, pushing the edges together and sealing firmly.
5. Gently drop dumplings into salted boiling water. Cook for 3 to 5 minutes, or until they float. (Approximately 1 1/2 to 2 dozen)
6. Prepare the cheese sauce by pressing the cheese through a sieve into a bowl. Add the remaining ingredients and mix.
Note: There is no explicit sauce preparation section in the original recipe. The instructions mention preparing a cheese sauce but do not provide specific steps for its creation.
